# Python MCQ Bank - PG Program in Data Analytics & Gen AI

## Quick Outline

Topics are taken from [prompt.txt](../prompt.txt). Topic links jump to the matching difficulty section.

- [Definition-Based Concept MCQs](#definition-concept-mcqs)
- [Beginner](#beginner-section) - [Core MCQs](#beginner-core) / [Scenario-Based Questions](#beginner-scenarios)
  Topics: [Variables, data types, operators](#beginner-section); [Control flow: if-else, for/while loops](#beginner-section); [Functions and basic syntax](#beginner-section); [Lists, tuples, dictionaries, sets](#beginner-section); [String manipulation](#beginner-section); [Basic file I/O](#beginner-section); [pip and package installation](#beginner-section); [Jupyter Notebook basics](#beginner-section)
- [Intermediate](#intermediate-section) - [Core MCQs](#intermediate-core) / [Scenario-Based Questions](#intermediate-scenarios)
  Topics: [NumPy: arrays, indexing, broadcasting](#intermediate-section); [Pandas: Series, DataFrame, filtering, groupby, merge/join](#intermediate-section); [Data cleaning (missing values, duplicates)](#intermediate-section); [Data visualization: Matplotlib, Seaborn basics](#intermediate-section); [Reading/writing CSV, Excel, JSON](#intermediate-section); [Descriptive statistics in Python (mean, median, std, correlation)](#intermediate-section); [List comprehensions](#intermediate-section); [Exception handling](#intermediate-section); [Lambda, map/filter/reduce](#intermediate-section); [Basic OOP (classes, objects)](#intermediate-section)
- [Advanced](#advanced-section) - [Core MCQs](#advanced-core) / [Scenario-Based Questions](#advanced-scenarios)
  Topics: [Advanced Pandas (multi-indexing, pivot tables, vectorization, performance)](#advanced-section); [Statistical modeling (SciPy, statsmodels)](#advanced-section); [Machine learning with scikit-learn (regression, classification, clustering, evaluation metrics)](#advanced-section); [Intro to Gen AI/NLP: tokenization, embeddings, transformer basics](#advanced-section); [Using LLM APIs and prompt engineering in code](#advanced-section); [RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) and LangChain concepts](#advanced-section); [Vector databases (conceptual understanding)](#advanced-section); [Web scraping (BeautifulSoup, requests)](#advanced-section); [Workflow automation/scripting](#advanced-section); [Git basics for Python projects](#advanced-section); [Real-life: cleaning duplicate/null transaction data](#advanced-section); [support-ticket chatbot using an LLM API](#advanced-section); [automated weekly stakeholder email reports](#advanced-section)

<a id="definition-concept-mcqs"></a>
## Definition-Based Concept MCQs

#### Q1. Which definition best describes variable? [Concept]

- **A.** A name that refers to a value or object in memory.
- **B.** A chart formatting option with no effect on code.
- **C.** A package password used only during installation.
- **D.** A file export setting used only for notebooks.

**Correct answer:** A

**Explanation:** A name that refers to a value or object in memory. This definition helps analysts use variable correctly in practical work.

#### Q2. Which definition best describes data type? [Concept]

- **A.** A chart formatting option with no effect on code.
- **B.** A classification that defines what kind of value an object holds.
- **C.** A package password used only during installation.
- **D.** A file export setting used only for notebooks.

**Correct answer:** B

**Explanation:** A classification that defines what kind of value an object holds. This definition helps analysts use data type correctly in practical work.

<a id="beginner-section"></a>
## Beginner

<a id="beginner-core"></a>
### Core MCQs (Concept + Tool-Usage)

#### Q1. Which statement best explains the role of Variables, data types, and operators in Python for UK data analytics work? [Concept]

- **A.** It improves reliability, interpretability, and auditability before decisions are made.
- **B.** It is mainly a visual styling choice with no effect on analytical quality.
- **C.** It guarantees that all source data is correct even when no validation is performed.
- **D.** It removes the need to agree metric definitions with stakeholders.

**Correct answer:** A

**Explanation:** Variables, data types, and operators matters because it improves the quality, traceability, and business meaning of the notebook, script, or pipeline. Analysts at this level are expected to validate and explain their work.

#### Q2. Which practical step best demonstrates correct use of Variables, data types, and operators in a notebook, script, or pipeline? [Tool-Usage]

- **A.** Assume the default output is correct and skip all checks against the source.
- **B.** Use the tool feature deliberately, check a small sample, and document any assumption in the notebook, script, or pipeline.
- **C.** Change every field to text to avoid thinking about data types or aggregation.
- **D.** Delete records that do not match the expected conclusion before investigating them.

**Correct answer:** B

**Explanation:** Correct use of Variables, data types, and operators means applying the right Python capability and checking it against unit checks, sample rows, summary statistics, logs, and reproducible outputs. That keeps the analysis repeatable and credible.

#### Q3. What is the strongest reason to understand Control flow: if-else, for loops, while loops before presenting analysis? [Concept]

- **A.** It removes the need to agree metric definitions with stakeholders.
- **B.** It should be used only after the report has already been published.
- **C.** It reduces ambiguity in definitions, calculations, and assumptions.
- **D.** It is useful only for personal practice files and not for team analytics work.

**Correct answer:** C

**Explanation:** Control flow: if-else, for loops, while loops matters because it improves the quality, traceability, and business meaning of the notebook, script, or pipeline. Analysts at this level are expected to validate and explain their work.

#### Q4. Which workflow is the best way to use Control flow: if-else, for loops, while loops in production-style analysis? [Tool-Usage]

- **A.** Delete records that do not match the expected conclusion before investigating them.
- **B.** Build the answer manually and avoid leaving a repeatable calculation path.
- **C.** Hide errors in the final output without checking why they occurred.
- **D.** Start with the business requirement, use the correct feature, and test the output before sharing it.

**Correct answer:** D

**Explanation:** Correct use of Control flow: if-else, for loops, while loops means applying the right Python capability and checking it against unit checks, sample rows, summary statistics, logs, and reproducible outputs. That keeps the analysis repeatable and credible.

#### Q5. How should Functions and basic syntax be treated? [Concept]

- **A.** It supports repeatable analysis rather than one-off manual judgement.
- **B.** It is useful only for personal practice files and not for team analytics work.
- **C.** It replaces the need to understand the business question.
- **D.** It is relevant only when the dataset contains fewer than one hundred rows.

**Correct answer:** A

**Explanation:** Functions and basic syntax matters because it improves the quality, traceability, and business meaning of the notebook, script, or pipeline. Analysts at this level are expected to validate and explain their work.

#### Q6. How should Functions and basic syntax be used so the result can be checked and repeated? [Tool-Usage]

- **A.** Hide errors in the final output without checking why they occurred.
- **B.** Create the output, inspect edge cases, and confirm that the result answers the stakeholder question.
- **C.** Prioritise cosmetic formatting before confirming the metric definition.
- **D.** Share the output immediately and wait for stakeholders to find any issues.

**Correct answer:** B

**Explanation:** Correct use of Functions and basic syntax means applying the right Python capability and checking it against unit checks, sample rows, summary statistics, logs, and reproducible outputs. That keeps the analysis repeatable and credible.

#### Q7. Which statement best connects Lists, tuples, dictionaries, sets to business decision-making? [Concept]

- **A.** It is relevant only when the dataset contains fewer than one hundred rows.
- **B.** It means the analyst no longer needs to document assumptions.
- **C.** It improves reliability, interpretability, and auditability before decisions are made.
- **D.** It is mainly a visual styling choice with no effect on analytical quality.

**Correct answer:** C

**Explanation:** Lists, tuples, dictionaries, sets matters because it improves the quality, traceability, and business meaning of the notebook, script, or pipeline. Analysts at this level are expected to validate and explain their work.

#### Q8. What is the best operational habit when working with Lists, tuples, dictionaries, sets? [Tool-Usage]

- **A.** Share the output immediately and wait for stakeholders to find any issues.
- **B.** Paste static values or screenshots so the result cannot be refreshed or audited.
- **C.** Assume the default output is correct and skip all checks against the source.
- **D.** Use the tool feature deliberately, check a small sample, and document any assumption in the notebook, script, or pipeline.

**Correct answer:** D

**Explanation:** Correct use of Lists, tuples, dictionaries, sets means applying the right Python capability and checking it against unit checks, sample rows, summary statistics, logs, and reproducible outputs. That keeps the analysis repeatable and credible.

- **D.** It removes the need to agree metric definitions with stakeholders.

**Correct answer:** A

**Explanation:** String manipulation matters because it improves the quality, traceability, and business meaning of the notebook, script, or pipeline. Analysts at this level are expected to validate and explain their work.

#### Q10. Which practical step best demonstrates correct use of String manipulation in a notebook, script, or pipeline? [Tool-Usage]

- **A.** Assume the default output is correct and skip all checks against the source.
- **B.** Start with the business requirement, use the correct feature, and test the output before sharing it.
- **C.** Change every field to text to avoid thinking about data types or aggregation.

<a id="beginner-scenarios"></a>
### Scenario-Based Questions - Beginner

#### Q1. You are working for a UK retail chain. A manager asks you to explain a small week-on-week KPI movement. The work involves Variables, data types, and operators in Python. What should you do first? [Scenario]

- **A.** Delete outliers or missing values before checking what they represent.
- **B.** Clarify the metric, inspect the relevant fields, apply Variables, data types, and operators in a controlled notebook, script, or pipeline, and validate the result before sharing.
- **C.** Change the visual styling and avoid challenging the metric definition.
- **D.** Use a manual workaround that cannot be repeated next month.

**Correct answer:** B

**Explanation:** This workplace scenario requires a controlled use of Variables, data types, and operators, plus definition checks, validation, and clear communication. That is the standard expected in UK analyst roles around GBP 45,000+.

#### Q2. A stakeholder at a London fintech team needs a quick but reliable answer to prepare a clean summary for a team meeting. The key skill is Control flow: if-else, for loops, while loops. What is the best next step? [Scenario]

- **A.** Change the visual styling and avoid challenging the metric definition.
- **B.** Use a manual workaround that cannot be repeated next month.
- **C.** Check the business question first, use Control flow: if-else, for loops, while loops on the right data, and compare the output with a simple trusted total.
- **D.** Bypass governance or access controls because the deadline is tight.

**Correct answer:** C

**Explanation:** This workplace scenario requires a controlled use of Control flow: if-else, for loops, while loops, plus definition checks, validation, and clear communication. That is the standard expected in UK analyst roles around GBP 45,000+.

#### Q3. You receive a small data extract from an NHS reporting team and need to check why two simple totals do not match. How should you use Functions and basic syntax? [Scenario]

- **A.** Use a manual workaround that cannot be repeated next month.
- **B.** Bypass governance or access controls because the deadline is tight.
- **C.** Optimise the final screen before checking source data grain or joins.
- **D.** Preserve the raw data, create a repeatable working view, and use Functions and basic syntax only after checking basic quality issues.

**Correct answer:** D

**Explanation:** This workplace scenario requires a controlled use of Functions and basic syntax, plus definition checks, validation, and clear communication. That is the standard expected in UK analyst roles around GBP 45,000+.

#### Q4. A non-technical colleague at a regional transport operator is unsure whether the output is trustworthy while you turn a raw export into a readable analyst note. Which approach is best? [Scenario]

- **A.** Ask what decision the output supports, apply Lists, tuples, dictionaries, sets, and explain any assumption in plain language.
- **B.** Bypass governance or access controls because the deadline is tight.
- **C.** Optimise the final screen before checking source data grain or joins.
- **D.** Assume another team has already validated the extract and skip reconciliation.

**Correct answer:** A

**Explanation:** This workplace scenario requires a controlled use of Lists, tuples, dictionaries, sets, plus definition checks, validation, and clear communication. That is the standard expected in UK analyst roles around GBP 45,000+.

#### Q5. You are working for a local council analytics team. A manager asks you to spot the main category driving a change in performance. The work involves String manipulation in Python. What should you do first? [Scenario]

- **A.** Optimise the final screen before checking source data grain or joins.
- **B.** Clarify the metric, inspect the relevant fields, apply String manipulation in a controlled notebook, script, or pipeline, and validate the result before sharing.
- **C.** Assume another team has already validated the extract and skip reconciliation.
- **D.** Hide caveats so the final answer looks more confident.

**Correct answer:** B

**Explanation:** This workplace scenario requires a controlled use of String manipulation, plus definition checks, validation, and clear communication. That is the standard expected in UK analyst roles around GBP 45,000+.

#### Q6. A stakeholder at a university planning office needs a quick but reliable answer to produce a basic view for a non-technical manager. The key skill is Basic file input and output. What is the best next step? [Scenario]

- **A.** Assume another team has already validated the extract and skip reconciliation.
- **B.** Hide caveats so the final answer looks more confident.
- **C.** Check the business question first, use Basic file input and output on the right data, and compare the output with a simple trusted total.
- **D.** Send the first result immediately so the stakeholder can decide whether it feels right.

**Correct answer:** C

## Intermediate

<a id="intermediate-core"></a>
### Core MCQs (Concept + Tool-Usage)

#### Q1. Which statement best explains the role of NumPy arrays, indexing, broadcasting in Python for UK data analytics work? [Concept]

- **A.** It improves reliability, interpretability, and auditability before decisions are made.
- **B.** It is mainly a visual styling choice with no effect on analytical quality.
- **C.** It guarantees that all source data is correct even when no validation is performed.
- **D.** It removes the need to agree metric definitions with stakeholders.

**Correct answer:** A

**Explanation:** NumPy arrays, indexing, broadcasting matters because it improves the quality, traceability, and business meaning of the notebook, script, or pipeline. Analysts at this level are expected to validate and explain their work.

#### Q2. Which practical step best demonstrates correct use of NumPy arrays, indexing, broadcasting in a notebook, script, or pipeline? [Tool-Usage]

- **A.** Assume the default output is correct and skip all checks against the source.
- **B.** Use the tool feature deliberately, check a small sample, and document any assumption in the notebook, script, or pipeline.
- **C.** Change every field to text to avoid thinking about data types or aggregation.
- **D.** Delete records that do not match the expected conclusion before investigating them.

**Correct answer:** B

**Explanation:** Correct use of NumPy arrays, indexing, broadcasting means applying the right Python capability and checking it against unit checks, sample rows, summary statistics, logs, and reproducible outputs. That keeps the analysis repeatable and credible.

### Scenario-Based Questions - Intermediate

#### Q1. You are supporting a UK retail chain and must prepare a monthly performance pack for finance and operations. Several teams disagree about the numbers, and NumPy arrays, indexing, broadcasting is central. What should you do? [Scenario]

- **A.** Delete outliers or missing values before checking what they represent.
- **B.** Agree definitions, profile the source data, build a repeatable solution using NumPy arrays, indexing, broadcasting, validate against source totals, and document caveats.
- **C.** Change the visual styling and avoid challenging the metric definition.
- **D.** Use a manual workaround that cannot be repeated next month.

**Correct answer:** B

**Explanation:** This workplace scenario requires a controlled use of NumPy arrays, indexing, broadcasting, plus definition checks, validation, and clear communication. That is the standard expected in UK analyst roles around GBP 45,000+.

- **D.** Bypass governance or access controls because the deadline is tight.

**Correct answer:** C

**Explanation:** This workplace scenario requires a controlled use of Pandas Series, DataFrame, filtering, groupby, merge, join, plus definition checks, validation, and clear communication. That is the standard expected in UK analyst roles around GBP 45,000+.

#### Q1. Which statement best explains the role of Advanced Pandas: multi-indexing, pivot tables, vectorization, performance in Python for UK data analytics work? [Concept]

- **A.** It improves reliability, interpretability, and auditability before decisions are made.
- **B.** It is mainly a visual styling choice with no effect on analytical quality.
- **C.** It guarantees that all source data is correct even when no validation is performed.
- **D.** It removes the need to agree metric definitions with stakeholders.

**Correct answer:** A

**Explanation:** Advanced Pandas: multi-indexing, pivot tables, vectorization, performance matters because it improves the quality, traceability, and business meaning of the notebook, script, or pipeline. Analysts at this level are expected to validate and explain their work.

#### Q2. Which practical step best demonstrates correct use of Advanced Pandas: multi-indexing, pivot tables, vectorization, performance in a notebook, script, or pipeline? [Tool-Usage]

- **C.** Change every field to text to avoid thinking about data types or aggregation.
- **D.** Delete records that do not match the expected conclusion before investigating them.

**Correct answer:** B

That keeps the analysis repeatable and credible.

### Scenario-Based Questions - Advanced (complex, multi-step, case-study style)

#### Q1. You lead an analytics workstream for a UK retail chain and must design an auditable executive reporting workflow under a tight deadline. The solution depends on Advanced Pandas: multi-indexing, pivot tables, vectorization, performance in Python. What is the best approach? [Scenario]

- **A.** Delete outliers or missing values before checking what they represent.
- **B.** Map requirements and data grain, design the solution around Advanced Pandas: multi-indexing, pivot tables, vectorization, performance, test security and performance, validate with owners, and publish with monitoring.
- **C.** Change the visual styling and avoid challenging the metric definition.
- **D.** Use a manual workaround that cannot be repeated next month.

**Correct answer:** B

**Explanation:** This workplace scenario requires a controlled use of Advanced Pandas: multi-indexing, pivot tables, vectorization, performance, plus definition checks, validation, and clear communication. That is the standard expected in UK analyst roles around GBP 45,000+.

- **B.** Hide caveats so the final answer looks more confident.
- **C.** Separate definition issues from tooling issues, use Git basics for Python projects in a governed workflow, test edge cases, and document the decision trail.
- **D.** Send the first result immediately so the stakeholder can decide whether it feels right.

**Correct answer:** C

**Explanation:** This workplace scenario requires a controlled use of Git basics for Python projects, plus definition checks, validation, and clear communication. That is the standard expected in UK analyst roles around GBP 45,000+.
